response.justification: The candidate is a versatile, experienced engineer with genuine developer-tooling credentials — he worked on the LabWindows/CVI IDE at National Instruments and has authored test runners and assertion libraries — but the role's core stack is essentially absent. His career centers on TypeScript/Node.js, DLang, and web platforms; Java appears only peripherally, and there is no Kotlin, no Spring or enterprise-framework work, and no evidence of deep JVM expertise, which are the non-negotiable requirements for this position.
strengths: Direct IDE development experience (C/C++ on NI LabWindows/CVI)
Authored language/dev tooling (Trial test runner, Fluent-Asserts) showing real interest in tooling internals
Long, varied track record across stacks with strong testing and craftsmanship culture (TDD, CI/CD)
concerns: No Kotlin and only minor, dated Java exposure; no demonstrated deep JVM knowledge
No Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or other enterprise Java framework experience
Recent work is web/full-stack (TypeScript, Node.js), far from enterprise JVM tooling
